reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly on multiple fronts. First, regarding Content Accuracy, the model hallucinates a substantial amount of text not present in the prompt. While it reads the target sentence (00:00-00:02), it continues with 'My voice is rough, like gravel in a windstorm, Horse from screaming into the dark' (00:02-00:07). This violates the instruction to simply read the specific sentence. Second, regarding the Core Paralinguistic Compliance (Timbre), the voice does not exhibit a 'hoarse' timbre. Instead, it uses a high-pitched, clear, and somewhat cartoonish or 'chibi' style voice. There is no audible graveliness, breathiness, or vocal strain associated with having shouted all night; in fact, the voice sounds energetic and clear, which directly contradicts the semantic meaning of 'now I can't speak'.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 performs exceptionally well. Regarding Content Accuracy, it reads the requested script perfectly without any additions or omissions (00:00-00:03). Regarding Paralinguistic Compliance (Timbre), the model successfully modifies the vocal characteristics to sound 'hoarse'. The audio features a breathy, whispered quality with distinct vocal fry and strain, effectively simulating the physical limitations of a voice that has been overused (laryngitis). The pacing is also appropriately slow and labored, enhancing the perception of the requested timbre. The effect is vivid and natural.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the negative constraint of reading *only* the provided text, adding a long hallucinated description. Furthermore, Model 1 failed to generate the target 'hoarse' timbre, producing a clear, high-pitched voice that sounded unrelated to the request. Model 2 followed the content instructions perfectly and delivered a high-quality, realistic simulation of a hoarse, strained voice that matched the semantic context of the script perfectly.
